NCT02164513.name: IMPACT
NCT02164513.group: Symptomatic COPD with >=1 exacerbation/y, FF/UMEC/VI vs UMEC/VI dual LAMA/LABA (52 wk)

allOutcomes.title: Moderate/severe exacerbation rate ratio (primary, vs LAMA/LABA)

NCT02164513.snippet: Source: Lipson DA et al. N Engl J Med 2018;378:1671-1680 (IMPACT).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 10,355 patients with symptomatic COPD and >=1 exacerbation in the prior year randomized 2:2:1 double-blind to fluticasone furoate / umeclidinium / vilanterol 100/62.5/25 mcg OD (n=4,151), FF/VI 100/25 mcg OD (n=4,134), or UMEC/VI 62.5/25 mcg OD (n=2,070) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - Moderate/Severe Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Annual rate of moderate/severe exacerbations: FF/UMEC/VI 0.91 vs UMEC/VI 1.21; rate ratio 0.75 (95% CI 0.70 to 0.81; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Severe Exacerbations, SGRQ, FEV1
evidence.text: Severe exacerbation rate vs LAMA/LABA: RR 0.66 (0.56-0.78). SGRQ change -1.8 vs LAMA/LABA (95% CI -2.4 to -1.2). Trough FEV1 +54 mL vs LAMA/LABA (95% CI +39 to +69). Pneumonia risk higher in ICS arms.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratification by prior exacerbations and smoking status. D2 LOW - double-blind, double-dummy with matched placebo inhalers. D3 LOW - 82% completion. D4 LOW - exacerbations centrally validated using pre-specified criteria. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Informed Consent: A signed and dated written informed consent prior to study participation~* Type of subject: Outpatient~* Age: Subjects 40 years of age or older at Visit 1~* Gender: Male or female subjects. A female is eligible to enter and participate in the study if she is of: Non-child bearing potential (i.e. physiologically incapable of becoming pregnant, including any female who is post-menopausal or surgically sterile). Surgically sterile females are defined as thos...

NCT02465567.name: ETHOS
NCT02465567.group: Symptomatic COPD with >=1 moderate or >=1 severe exacerbation/y, BGF 320 vs GFF dual LAMA/LABA (52 wk)

allOutcomes.title: Moderate/severe exacerbation rate ratio (primary, vs LAMA/LABA)

NCT02465567.snippet: Source: Rabe KF et al. N Engl J Med 2020;383:35-48 (ETHOS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 8,509 patients with symptomatic COPD with history of >=1 moderate or >=1 severe exacerbation in prior year randomized 1:1:1:1 double-blind to budesonide/glycopyrrolate/formoterol 320/18/9.6 mcg BID (BGF 320; n=2,137), BGF 160 (n=2,121), glyco/form (GFF; n=2,120), or bud/form (BFF; n=2,131) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - Moderate/Severe Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Annual rate of moderate/severe exacerbations: BGF 320 1.08 vs GFF 1.42; rate ratio 0.76 (95% CI 0.69 to 0.83; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met for both BGF doses.

evidence.label: Secondary - Severe Exacerbations, SGRQ, All-Cause Mortality
evidence.text: Severe exacerbation rate vs LAMA/LABA: RR 0.80 (0.63-1.00). SGRQ change -2.1 vs GFF (95% CI -3.0 to -1.2). All-cause mortality HR 0.53 (0.32-0.87) for BGF 320 vs GFF (pre-specified).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central stratified randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind, double-dummy MDI. D3 LOW - 83% completion. D4 LOW - exacerbations centrally validated. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria~* Non-child bearing potential (ie, physiologically incapable of becoming pregnant, including any female who is 2 years post-menopausal); or Child bearing potential, has a negative serum pregnancy test at Visit 1, and agrees to acceptable contraceptive methods used consistently and correctly for the duration of the study.~* Subjects with an established clinical history of COPD as defined by the American Thoracic Society (ATS)/European Respiratory Society (ERS),~* Current or fo...

NCT02497001.name: KRONOS
NCT02497001.group: Symptomatic moderate-very-severe COPD (not required exacerbator), BGF vs BFF dual ICS/LABA (24 wk)

allOutcomes.title: Moderate/severe exacerbation rate ratio vs ICS/LABA (primary ancillary)
Trough FEV1 change vs GFF (primary lung-function)

NCT02497001.snippet: Source: Ferguson GT et al. Lancet Respir Med 2018;6:747-758 (KRONOS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,896 patients with moderate-to-very-severe COPD (exacerbation history not required) randomized 2:2:1:1 double-blind to BGF 320/18/9.6 mcg BID (n=639), GFF dual LAMA/LABA (n=625), BFF dual ICS/LABA (n=314), or open-label Symbicort (n=318) for 24 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary Lung Function - Trough FEV1 vs GFF
evidence.text: Trough FEV1 change at week 24 vs GFF: BGF +22 mL (95% CI 0 to 44; P=0.0527; did not meet primary). BGF +74 mL vs BFF (95% CI 52-95, P<0.0001). BGF +116 mL vs open-label Sym (P<0.0001).

evidence.label: Secondary - Exacerbation Rate vs ICS/LABA
evidence.text: Moderate/severe exacerbation rate vs BFF (ICS/LABA): rate ratio 0.52 (95% CI 0.40 to 0.69; P<0.0001). BGF 0.46 events/year vs BFF 0.95 events/year. Favoured triple over ICS/LABA.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central stratified randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind for BGF/GFF/BFF arms; Symbicort arm was open-label (excluded from blinded comparisons). D3 LOW - 91% completion. D4 LOW - exacerbations centrally validated. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria~* Given their signed written informed consent to participate.~* Non-child bearing potential (ie, physiologically incapable of becoming pregnant, including any female who is 2 years post-menopausal); or Child bearing potential, has a negative serum pregnancy test at Visit 1, and agrees to acceptable contraceptive methods used consistently and correctly for the duration of the study.~* Subjects with an established clinical history of COPD as defined by the American Thoracic Soc...

title: RapidMeta Respiratory | COPD Triple Therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA) v1.0
